title: tephroseris integrifolia var. leiocarpa
intro: Tephroseris integrifolia var. leiocarpa, also known as the mountain everlast or alpine dandelion, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the daisy family. It is rich in medicinal properties, particularly in its leaves and roots, which contain flavonoids, sesquiterpene lactones, and other bioactive compounds. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and infections. Herbal preparations of Tephroseris integrifolia var. leiocarpa can be made by infusing its leaves and roots in hot water or by using them in teas, tinctures, and salves, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
